records.ety: [F. tenant, p. pr. of tenir to hold. See Tenable, and cf. Lieutenant.]

defs: One who holds or possesses lands, or other real estate, by any kind of right, whether in fee simple, in common, in severalty, for life, for years, or at will; also, one who has the occupation or temporary possession of lands or tenements the title of which is in another; -- correlative to landlord. See Citation from Blackstone, under Tenement, 2.
One who has possession of any place; a dweller; an occupant.
quotes: The hhappy tenant of your shade.
The sister tenants of the middle deep.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tenanted; p. pr. & vb. n. Tenanting.]

defs: To hold, occupy, or possess as a tenant.
quotes: Sir Roger's estate is tenanted by persons who have served him or his ancestors.
